title: Rigiolepis linearifolia
taxonomy: Rigiolepis linearifolia is classified within the family Ericaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as heath or heather family. This family comprises over 4,000 species of plants and is widely distributed across the globe. The genus Rigiolepis itself is a relatively small group of flowering plants, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant's classification within the Ericaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other similar plant species.

intro: Rigiolepis linearifolia, also known as the "Linear-leaved Rigiolepis," is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating infections, wounds, and other health conditions.
